free_text: Fusion of 19-F with 188, 192-Os
Nuclear physics and radiochemistry divisions
Targets used in this measurement were 0.5 mg/cm**2 to
1.5 mg/cm**2 thick and were prepared by
electro-deposition on ~1 mg/cm**2 Cu backings. Target
thickness values were estimated by measuring Rutherford
scattering.
Relative thickness values of different targets were
also checked by measuring X-ray induced fluorescence.
While 192-Os targets were 99% enriched, 188-Os targets
had the following isotopic composition, 86.7% 188-Os,
Pelletron accelerator
Data tables are received from K.Mahata
Last checking has been done.
From K.Mahata
Most of the fission fragments were stopped in the
delta-E detector.   Fragments  reaching the E detector
were well separated from the direct reaction products
and evaporated particles in two-dimensional E vs.
delta-E Plots.
Three delta-E - e telescopes
consisting of Si surface barrier detectors (thickness
delta-E  = 10-15 mumeters, E = 300 mumeters) were used
to detect fission fragments.
Two Si surface barrier detectors were kept at
35 and 55 degrees to monitor Rutherford scattering
necessary for absolute normalization of the fission
cross-sections.
Errors for fission cross section include
uncertainties in solid angle and counting statistics.
Coupled channels code CCDEF.
CCDEF Code

Irradiation times were varied between 30
minutes to 3 hours according to the half life of the
evaporation residues that were expected to be formed in
significant abundance.
The beam current was kept around 10 particle
nanoampere and monitored in a multi-scaling mode to
take into account the fluctuation in beam intensity
with time.
After irradiation, the targets were removed and
beam induced activity was counted using an efficiency
calibrated 60 cc HPGe detector for several half lives
of the evaporation residues produced.
The errors in evaporation residue cross
sections include uncertainties in gamma-ray branching
ratio, half-life, detector efficiency, target thickness
and coumting statistics.
Excitation energy of compound nucleus (At-207)

-By authors.  In the case of 203-At , absolute
intensities of gamma-rays were not available.   Hence,
the activity of 203-Po (daughter nuclei from beta-decay
of  203-At)  was followed.  Small corrections due to
direct formation of 203-Po were made using statistical
model predictions, which explain all other major
channels satisfactorily.
Contributions of other isotopes present in small
abundance, in the case of 188-Os target, were also
corrected using statistical model predictions.
